answer:
We can use put-call symmetry for currency options. The relationship is:

\[
P_d(S, K, T) = K \cdot P_f\left(\frac{1}{K}, \frac{1}{S}, T\right)
\]

Here, the dollar-denominated put on yen has \(K = 0.008\) $/¥, price \(0.0005\) $, and spot \(0.011\) $/¥. The yen-denominated put on dollars has strike \(125\) ¥/$ (since \(1/0.008 = 125\)). Converting the put price:

\[
\text{Put price in ¥} = \frac{0.0005}{0.011} \times 125 \approx 5.6818
\]

But we must also adjust for interest rate differences using the symmetry formula:

\[
P_{\text{yen}}(125) = 125 \times P_{\text{dollar}}(0.008) \times e
